corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

location.sentence: In this work, we show that late interaction re- trievers naturally produce lightweight token rep- resentations that are amenable to efficient storage off-the-shelf and that they can benefit drastically from denoised supervision.

context_window.previous: Indeed, recent single-vector models with highly- tuned supervision strategies (Ren et al., 2021b; For- mal et al., 2021a) sometimes perform on-par or even better than “vanilla” late interaction models, and it is not necessarily clear whether late inter- action architectures—with their fixed token-level inductive biases—admit similarly large gains from improved supervision.
context_window.current: In this work, we show that late interaction re- trievers naturally produce lightweight token rep- resentations that are amenable to efficient storage off-the-shelf and that they can benefit drastically from denoised supervision.
context_window.next: We couple those in ColBERTv2 , 1 a new late-interaction retriever that employs a simple combination of distillation from 1 Code, models, and LoTTE data are maintained at https: //github.com/stanford-futuredata/ColBERT arXiv:2112.01488v3 [cs.IR] 10 Jul 2022 a cross-encoder and hard-negative mining (§3.2) to boost quality beyond any existing method, and then uses a residual compression mechanism (§3.3) to reduce the space footprint of late interaction by 6–10 × while preserving quality.

location.sentence: This added expressivity comes at a cost: existing late interaction systems impose an order-of-magnitude larger space footprint than single-vector models, as they must store billions of small vectors for Web-scale collections.

context_window.previous: By decomposing relevance modeling into token- level computations, late interaction aims to reduce the burden on the encoder: whereas single-vector models must capture complex query–document re- lationships within one dot product, late interaction encodes meaning at the level of tokens and del- egates query–document matching to the interac- tion mechanism.
context_window.current: This added expressivity comes at a cost: existing late interaction systems impose an order-of-magnitude larger space footprint than single-vector models, as they must store billions of small vectors for Web-scale collections.
context_window.next: Con- sidering this challenge, it might seem more fruit- ful to focus instead on addressing the fragility of single-vector models (Menon et al., 2022) by in- troducing new supervision paradigms for negative mining (Xiong et al., 2020), pretraining (Gao and Callan, 2021), and distillation (Qu et al., 2021).

location.sentence: During offline indexing, every passage d in the corpus is encoded into a set of vectors, and these vectors are stored.

context_window.previous: Queries and pas- sages are independently encoded with BERT (De- vlin et al., 2019), and the output embeddings encod- ing each token are projected to a lower dimension.
context_window.current: During offline indexing, every passage d in the corpus is encoded into a set of vectors, and these vectors are stored.
context_window.next: At search time, the query q is encoded into a multi-vector representation, and its similarity to a passage d is computed as the summa- tion of query-side “MaxSim” operations, namely, the largest cosine similarity between each query to- ken embedding and all passage token embeddings: S q,d = N ∑ i =1 M max j =1 Q i · D T j (1) where Q is an matrix encoding the query with N vectors and D encodes the passage with M vectors.

top_candidates.sentence: We expect our approach to work effectively for other languages and when all models are trained using other, smaller training set (e.g., NaturalQuestions), but we leave such tests to future work.

However, we caution that empirical trends can change as innovations are introduced to each of these families of models and that it can be difficult to ensure exact apple-to-apple comparisons across families of models, since each of them calls for different sophisticated tuning strategies.
ColBERTv2 adopts the late interaction architecture of ColBERT, depicted in Figure .
The intuition of this architecture is to align each query token with the most contextually relevant passage token, quantify these matches, and combine the partial scores across the query.
In §4, we introduce LoTTE, a new dataset for out-of-domain retrieval, exhibiting natural search queries over long-tail topics.
We introduce LoTTE (pronounced latte), a new dataset for Long-Tail Topic-stratified Evaluation for IR.
In the pooled setting, the passages and queries are aggregated across all test (or dev) topics to evaluate out-of-domain retrieval across a larger and more diverse corpus.
To evaluate retrievers, we collect Search and Forum queries, each of which is associated with one or more target answer posts in its corpus.
We collect search queries from GooAQ , a recent dataset of Google search-autocomplete queries and their answer boxes, which we filter for queries whose answers link to a specific StackExchange post.
This analysis indicates that cluster centroids can summarize the ColBERT representations with high precision.

This out-of-domain regime has received recent attention with the BEIR benchmark.

In this work, we introduce ColBERTv2, a retriever that couples an aggressive residual compression mechanism with a denoised supervision strategy to simultaneously improve the quality and space footprint of late interaction.

We evaluate ColBERTv2 across a wide range of benchmarks, establishing state-of-the-art quality within and outside the training domain while reducing the space footprint of late interaction models by 6-10×.

Considering this challenge, it might seem more fruitful to focus instead on addressing the fragility of single-vector models by introducing new supervision paradigms for negative mining , pretraining , and distillation .
As a result, Col-BERTv2 establishes state-of-the-art retrieval quality both within and outside its training domain with a competitive space footprint with typical singlevector models.
When trained on MS MARCO Passage Ranking, ColBERTv2 achieves the highest MRR@10 of any standalone retriever.
When trained on MS MARCO Passage Ranking, ColBERTv2 achieves the highest MRR@10 of any standalone retriever.
When trained on MS MARCO Passage Ranking, ColBERTv2 achieves the highest MRR@10 of any standalone retriever.

In addition, we introduce a new benchmark, dubbed LoTTE, for Long-Tail Topic-stratified Evaluation for IR that features 12 domain-specific search tests, spanning StackExchange communities and using queries from GooAQ .

In addition, we introduce a new benchmark, dubbed LoTTE, for Long-Tail Topic-stratified Evaluation for IR that features 12 domain-specific search tests, spanning StackExchange communities and using queries from GooAQ .
On 22 of 28 out-of-domain tests, ColBERTv2 achieves the highest quality, outperforming the next best retriever by up to 8% relative gain, while using its compressed representations.

We propose ColBERTv2, a retriever that combines denoised supervision and residual compression, leveraging the token-level decomposition of late interaction to achieve high robustness with a reduced space footprint.
We evaluate ColBERTv2 across a wide range of settings, establishing state-of-the-art quality within and outside the training domain.

downstream_contract.intended_use: Feed this JSON to a later local open-source model to write an overview.
downstream_contract.grounding_rule: The downstream generator should write from evidence_units.location.sentence and context_window, not from unsupported outside knowledge.
downstream_contract.score_warning: Scores are ranking and filtering signals, not calibrated probabilities.
